## Model Context Protocol

Slug: model-context-protocol
Type: workflow
Trust tier: verified
Review status: manual-reviewed
Freshness: current
Source confidence: official
Recommendation score: 13
Protocols: MCP
Clients: Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, OpenAI Agents SDK
Tags: mcp, protocol, tools, resources, prompts, context
Use cases: Standardize agent access to tools and context, Compare MCP servers by permissions and provenance
Best for: Teams that need portable tool connections across agent clients, Standardize agent access to tools and context, Compare MCP servers by permissions and provenance, mcp workflows, protocol workflows, tools workflows, resources workflows, prompts workflows
Recommended when: Use when agent tools should be discoverable, permissioned, and reusable across clients., Use when a project needs standardize agent access to tools and context and the upstream source has been reviewed., Prefer this when verified install and behavior notes are required.
Risk flags: none recorded
Homepage: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/getting-started/intro
Repository: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol
Sources: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/getting-started/intro, https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol

Model Context Protocol is an open protocol for connecting AI applications to external tools, resources, prompts, and contextual data through standardized servers.

Model Context Protocol is an open protocol for connecting AI applications to external tools, resources, prompts, and contextual data through standardized servers. ## Best For - Teams that need portable tool connections across agent clients ## Recommended When - Use when agent tools should be discoverable, permissioned,

Clients: Agent runtimes

Sources: https://developers.googleblog.com/en/a2a-a-new-era-of-agent-interoperability/, https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A

Agent2Agent Protocol is a Google-backed open protocol for agent interoperability, enabling agents and agent runtimes to communicate across vendors and systems.

## vnem MCP

Slug: vnem-mcp
Type: mcp-server
Trust tier: verified
Review status: manual-reviewed
Freshness: current
Source confidence: official
Recommendation score: 11
Protocols: MCP
Clients: Codex, Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, OpenAI Agents SDK
Tags: vnem, mcp, registry, agent-tools, best-practices, prompt-patterns, read-only
Use cases: Give coding agents vnem search and recommendation tools, Query current MCP, skill, memory, eval, prompt, and coding-agent guidance, Compare registry entries with trust and permission notes
Best for: Repositories that want local, source-backed tool recommendations without granting mutation permissions, Agents that should consult vnem before choosing MCP servers, memory layers, skills, or search tools, Give coding agents vnem search and recommendation tools, Query current MCP, skill, memory, eval, prompt, and coding-agent guidance, Compare registry entries with trust and permission notes, vnem workflows, mcp workflows, registry workflows
Recommended when: Use when a coding agent should query vnem through MCP instead of manually reading `.vnem/search-index.json`., Use when recommendations need trust tiers, install notes, permissions, risk flags, and source URLs., Use when a project needs give coding agents vnem search and recommendation tools and the upstream source has been reviewed., Prefer this when verified install and behavior notes are required.
Risk flags: reads-local-files, sensitive-permissions
Homepage: https://github.com/naellisim/vnem
Repository: https://github.com/naellisim/vnem
Sources: https://github.com/naellisim/vnem, https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk

vnem MCP is the local read-only MCP server for the vnem registry, exposing generated search data, best-practice notes, prompt patterns, and registry entries to coding agents before they choose tools or upgrade paths.

vnem MCP is the local read-only MCP server for the vnem registry. It exposes vnem search, recommendation, comparison, entry lookup, and best-practice guidance to agent clients over stdio. ## Best For - Coding agents that should consult vnem before choosing MCP servers, skills, memory layers, prompt patterns, evals, sea

KAPLAY is a JavaScript and TypeScript 2D game library for fast browser-game prototyping. It is most useful when an agent needs a small playable game quickly, with simple game objects, collisions, input, sprites, and concise examples. ## Best For - Fast 2D browser-game prototypes where concise game code matters most ##

Homepage: https://run.mcp.com.ai
Repository: none recorded
Sources: https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io/v0.1/servers/ai.com.mcp%2Flinkedin/versions/latest, https://github.com/la-rebelion/hapimcp, https://run.mcp.com.ai

LinkedIn MCP Server is an MCP server for agentic systems. LinkedIn API as MCP tools to retrieve profile data and publish content. Powered by HAPI MCP.

PixiJS is a lightweight 2D rendering library for rich browser graphics and interactive scenes. It is best treated as a renderer rather than a complete game framework: useful when the game needs fast sprites, filters, textures, and interaction, but the agent should keep rules and state architecture explicit. ## Best For
